findings.detail: All imports from stdlib only (hashlib, re, dataclasses, datetime). No imports from marketmind modules — zero back-import risk.
Single public function extract_predictions(hypotheses: list) -> list[PredictableHypothesis]. Clean contract with well-defined input/output types.

Input: list of HypothesisResult objects (from HVR investigation loop). Output: list of PredictableHypothesis dataclass instances. No side effects, no global state mutation. Pure function — deterministic given same input.
Standalone module — no existing callers to break. Consumed by learning_store.save_prediction() and calibration_tracker.track_calibration().

notes: Clean extract-module pattern. No dependencies on glue layer or sibling modules. Dataclass PredictableHypothesis is self-contained. The module is importable by any consumer that passes HypothesisResult-like objects with .hypothesis, .direction, .time_window, .verdict, .confidence attributes.
